compiled_state.title: As a developer implementing AST-based kudos triggers, I can trust Python AST subtree hashing for reliable duplicate block detection
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Spike S1 — validate Python AST subtree hashing (hashlib.sha256(ast.dump(node).encode())) for duplicate code detection, measure false-positive rate, and confirm jscpd file-output integration as fallback.
**Why**: Story F (T13) detects >= 3 duplicate AST subtrees removed. The algorithm and threshold must be validated before implementation to avoid shipping a trigger with an unacceptable false-positive rate.
**Scope**:
- IN: Python prototype, false-positive measurement, jscpd file-output confirmation, algorithm/threshold recommendation
- OUT: T13 trigger implementation itself (Story F)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a Python prototype using hashlib.sha256(ast.dump(node).encode()) is tested against >= 5 known duplicate and non-duplicate sample pairs
  ← Satisfies: SC-E1-12
- When this story is complete, the false-positive rate is documented: percentage of non-duplicate subtrees that hash identically across the sample set
  ← Satisfies: SC-E1-12
- When this story is complete, jscpd file-output integration is confirmed working: REPORT_DIR=/tmp/tmp.oBvP28soMP && jscpd --reporters json --output  <files> && cat /jscpd-report.json (NOT stdout)
  ← Satisfies: SC-E1-12
- When this story is complete, an explicit recommendation is recorded: AST hashing vs jscpd for T13, with rationale (false-positive rate, dependency cost, reliability)
  ← Satisfies: SC-E1-12
- When this story is complete, results are recorded as a ticket comment: algorithm choice, threshold, jscpd approach, false-positive rate
  ← Satisfies: SC-E1-12
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for the spike prototype

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. High confidence means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.
